bill.short_title: Extension of Continuing Appropriations and Other Matters Act, 2024

provisions.description: Extends the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting March 22, 2024.

metadata.llm_classification: continuing_resolution_date_extension — amends section 106(3) of P.L. 118-15 to extend the CR end date to March 22, 2024

provisions.raw_text: (1) by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting ''March 22, 2024''
notes: Section 106(3) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of P.L. 118-15) specifies the general CR expiration date. This paragraph extends it to March 22, 2024.

cross_references.target: Section 106(3) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of Public Law 118-15)
cross_references.description: Extends the CR expiration date to March 22, 2024

provisions.description: Amends the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 by striking the date specified in section 106(4) and inserting March 8, 2024.

metadata.llm_classification: continuing_resolution_date_extension — amends section 106(4) of P.L. 118-15 to change the secondary CR date to March 8, 2024

provisions.raw_text: (2) by striking the date specified in section 106(4) and inserting ''March 8, 2024''
notes: Section 106(4) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of P.L. 118-15) specifies a secondary date, typically a staggered expiration for certain divisions. This paragraph sets it to March 8, 2024.
The two different dates (March 8 and March 22) create a laddered CR expiration, with some appropriations bills expiring earlier than others.

cross_references.target: Section 106(4) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of Public Law 118-15)
cross_references.description: Changes the secondary CR expiration date to March 8, 2024

provisions.description: Amends the FAFSA student available income formula in Section 475(g)(1) of the Higher Education Act of 1965, setting the income assessment at 50 percent of adjusted income with a floor of -$1,500 for award year 2024-2025 and zero for 2025-2026 and thereafter.
provisions.raw_text: Section 475(g)(1) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1087oo(g)(1)), as amended by the FAFSA Simplification Act (title VII of division FF o
notes: Subsection (a). Modifies the student available income calculation formula to allow a negative floor of -$1,500 for award year 2024-2025, effectively increasing aid eligibility.

cross_references.target: Section 475(g)(1) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1087oo(g)(1))
cross_references.description: As previously amended by the FAFSA Simplification Act (title VII of division FF of P.L. 116-260)

notes: Subsection (b). Clause (iv) of the amended Section 401(b)(7)(A). This is a permanent annual mandatory appropriation of $1.236B starting in FY2027.

notes: Subsection (c). Ensures that the new mandatory Pell Grant appropriations do not reduce or replace previously enacted discretionary appropriations for FY2024.

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from the Statutory PAYGO scorecards maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010, from Senate PAYGO scorecards under H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress), and from classification under the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act for purposes of section 251 sequestration, 302(a) allocations, and Statutory PAYGO scoring.
provisions.raw_text: The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
notes: Standard PAYGO exemption provision ensuring the mandatory Pell Grant spending in this division does not trigger sequestration or create points of order. Covers both statutory PAYGO (P.L. 111-139), Senate PAYGO (H. Con. Res. 71), and BCA section 251 caps/302(a) allocations.

cross_references.target: Section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)

cross_references.description: Exempts from Rule 3 of scorekeeping guidelines

cross_references.description: Not estimated for discretionary spending limits

Section 3(4)(C) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010

flagged_issues: Division A contains no new dollar amounts — it solely extends the dates of the existing CR (P.L. 118-15, Division A).
The laddered dates (March 8 vs March 22, 2024) correspond to sections 106(4) and 106(3) respectively, creating a staggered expiration for different sets of appropriations bills.
SEC. 101(b) creates permanent mandatory appropriations for Pell Grants totaling $7.746B over FY2024-2027 plus $1.236B/year thereafter
SEC. 102 exempts all Division B spending from PAYGO enforcement, meaning the $7.746B+ in new mandatory spending will not trigger sequestration
The FY2027 amount of $1,236,000,000 applies to 'each succeeding fiscal year' — a permanent indefinite-duration mandatory appropriation
